signal_title: European Commission fines X €120 million for transparency violations
risk_rationale: The European Commission's fine highlights significant compliance gaps in transparency practices, which are critical under the DSA. This poses a material compliance risk for TikTok if similar practices are found.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Ad transparency controls were under formal regulatory challenge.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Ensure full transparency in advertising practices, including accurate verification and disclosure.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential gap in advertising transparency and verification practices as highlighted by the fine imposed on another entity.

remediations.title: Enhance Advertising Transparency
remediations.description: Implement comprehensive transparency measures for advertising, ensuring accurate verification processes and clear disclosure of ad content, targeting criteria, and audience data.
acceptance_criteria: All ads include clear disclosure of content and targeting criteria.
Verification processes are documented and transparent.
Regular audits of ad transparency practices are conducted.

business_impact: Failure to address these transparency issues could result in significant fines and damage to TikTok's reputation in the EU market.
recommended_actions: Conduct an internal audit of current advertising transparency practices.
Develop and implement a plan to address identified gaps.
Engage with EU regulators to ensure compliance with DSA requirements.
